relations.note: Mandatory targets refine the general bolding budget allowed per section.

quotes: Bold is for bold is for hierarchy and key takeaways, not decoration.
Per email section, bold at most: the heading(s), one key phrase or claim per paragraph, and the statistical callout figure.
Never bold: entire paragraphs, footnotes/references, more than ~15% of running body text in any section

rules.intent: Maintain strict typographical hierarchy and prevent visual noise from over-bolding.
rules.rule_text: Bold must be used strictly for hierarchy and key takeaways, not decoration. Per email section, bold at most: the heading(s), one key phrase or claim per paragraph, and the statistical callout figure. Never bold entire paragraphs, footnotes/references, or more than approximately 15% of running body text in any section.

quotes: Always bold: callout text per the callout specs (§1.7), statistical callout numbers, brand name within a benefit claim when it is the focal point of the sentence (first instance per section only).
Never bold: ... prepositional filler, the generic name.

rules.intent: Highlight key quantitative data and brand focus consistently across layouts while protecting legal guidelines for generic names.
rules.rule_text: Always bold: callout text per the callout specs, statistical callout numbers, and the brand name within a benefit claim when it is the focal point of the sentence (restricted to the first instance per section only). Never bold the generic name or prepositional filler.

quotes: Never use bold + ALL CAPS + color emphasis simultaneously on the same string except in chart headlines and the campaign headline lockup. Pick at most two emphasis devices per element.

rules.intent: Avoid visual over-emphasis and clashing styles on single elements.
rules.rule_text: Never use bold + ALL CAPS + color emphasis simultaneously on the same string except in chart headlines and the campaign headline lockup. Pick at most two emphasis devices per element.

quotes: No underlining except real hyperlinks. No italic for emphasis in patient copy — reserve italics for study names, notes ("Note:"), and citations.

rules.intent: Avoid confusing standard emphasized text with hyperlinks, and maintain readability standard for patient copy.
rules.rule_text: No underlining is allowed unless it is a real hyperlink (body.link.text_decoration = underline). No italics for emphasis in patient copy; reserve italics strictly for study names, citations, and notes ('Note:').
